checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains headings and text that are directly present in the source PDF. Each slide from the source is represented with its corresponding text. There are no fabricated paragraphs or invented section content outside of the acceptable AI-generated metadata and titles used for document structure.

The output correctly identifies and reproduces the social media handles (@NCDIT, @BroadbandIO, @ncicenter) and the URL (it.nc.gov) present on the final slide of the document. No fabricated phone numbers, emails, or URLs were detected.
All 15 pages (slides) from the source document are accounted for in the output JSON. The content from each goal and the final contact slide is present.
Every table present in the source PDF (pages 4-14) is represented in the JSON with the correct number of data rows. For example, Goal 1.7 (page 10) correctly contains two data rows in its table.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The text content within each section matches the source PDF. The goals, benefits, and actions are all fully captured without significant omissions.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the JSON faithfully represents the text in the source document. Punctuation and formatting differences (like combining multi-line headers) are within the acceptable range for accessibility-oriented conversion. Meaning is preserved throughout.

checks.a_analysis: The document title 'GICC Goals and Strategic Direction 2021-2023' appears only once in the source (Page 1), but is used as the title for both Section 0 and Section 11 in the output. Additionally, for Goals 1.1 through 1.7 (Sections 3-9), the slide titles are duplicated: once as the section title and again as the content of the heading element (e.g., Section 3 has title 'Goal 1.1' and heading 'Goal 1.1. Promote...'). This results in the text 'Goal 1.1' appearing more times in the output than in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Goal 1.1. Promote free and open discovery of and access to geospatial data created and maintained by local governments.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section title: "Goal 1.1", Element heading: "Goal 1.1. Promote free and open discovery of and access to geospatial data created and maintained by local governments."

checks.a_analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent across the document. Slide titles for Goal 1.1 through 1.7 are assigned level 3 headings. However, slide titles for Goal 2.2 through 2.4 and 'Let’s Connect!' (all located inside Section 11) are assigned level 2 headings. These items are peers in the document structure and should be at the same level. Furthermore, in Section 10 (Goal 2.1), the slide title is not represented as a heading element at all, unlike previous sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: Slide titles on pages 4-10 (Goal 1.x) vs slides on pages 12-15 (Goal 2.x, Connect).
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 3 element 0 is level 3 heading; Section 11 element 0 is level 2 heading.

checks.a_analysis: All tables in the document (Goal 1.1 through 2.4) are correctly identified and structured with appropriate header rows and data cells corresponding to the two-column 'Benefit/Action' format in the source.
The reading order follows the page sequence and internal slide content logically from top to bottom.
Unordered lists on the Timeline slide (Page 2) and Goals slide (Page 3) are correctly identified and the items are preserved.
The URL 'it.nc.gov' from page 15 is captured as text. No fabricated links or broken links were introduced.

checks.a_analysis: The converted HTML document correctly translates the slide deck into a linear long-form document. The organization of information follows the source PDF page-by-page. Information can be found in the same relative order. The single-column layout is an expected conversion for HTML and does not confuse the meaning.

The tables present on pages 4 through 14 (Goals 1.1 to 2.4) are rendered correctly in the HTML output. The 'Benefit' and 'Action' headers are clearly distinguished from the body rows, and the cell content is aligned and readable. No visually corrupted tables were found.

There are significant issues with image rendering. First, the primary logo on the title page (Source Page 1) is missing its text component ('DIT') in the HTML render, showing only the 'NC' and nodes graphic. Second, the social media brand icons (Twitter, YouTube, Facebook, LinkedIn, Flickr) on the 'Let’s Connect!' slide (Source Page 15) are replaced by solid green placeholder boxes. These are meaningful icons as they identify the social platforms associated with the provided text handles (especially LinkedIn and Flickr where the text does not name the platform). While decorative logo repeats being hidden is acceptable, the title logo and service icons are meaningful content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1 shows a large logo with 'NC' and 'DIT' text; Page 15 shows icons for Twitter, YouTube, Facebook, LinkedIn, and Flickr.
checks.b_evidence_output: The top of the HTML shows the title graphic without the 'DIT' text. The bottom section of the HTML shows solid green boxes instead of social media icons.
checks.b_evidence_location: Title page area (Element index 2/3) and 'Let’s Connect!' section at the end of the document.

checks.a_analysis: No body content (paragraphs or tables) is visually duplicated. The repetition of the Goal footers (e.g., 'Goal 1: Improve, expand...') correctly reflects the source material where this text appears at the bottom of multiple consecutive slides. The presence of headings in both the Table of Contents and the body is an expected accessibility feature.
